response.justification: While Bogdan Szabo brings over 20 years of broad software development experience and strong software craftsmanship practices, the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise (8–12 years) for high-performance industrial systems — a requirement the candidate clearly does not meet. C++ appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2007–2008) and a university thesis project, with no demonstrable experience in STL-heavy systems programming, multi-threaded C++ architectures, message brokers, MQTT, or industrial cybersecurity standards such as IEC 62443. The candidate's primary technical identity is TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web-oriented full-stack work, which is fundamentally misaligned with the core competency profile of this role.
strengths: 20+ years of professional experience with consistently demonstrated software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, agile)
Practical background in event-driven and distributed architectures, REST APIs, and scalable platform design
History of mentoring, technical knowledge-sharing (workshops, public talks), and community contribution
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and confined to a single 6-month role in 2007–2008 and a thesis project, representing roughly 0.5 years versus the 8–12 required
No evidence of MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards
No background in industrial automation, embedded systems at scale, or on-premises manufacturing platform development

key_factors.factor: No industrial domain or ICS/SCADA exposure
